text: The Undead Siege

One of the last of the Draal Queens resides in this fetid swamp. The rise of the undead has driven away many of the humans and other wildlife of the region, giving the Draal an opportunity to regrow their numbers and begin to establish a true hive. You must end this infestation before it devours the entire region.
Slay the draal queen in Trollmud Swamp.
You have slain the queen of the draal
You have slain the draal queen. As it died, time itself shuddered around you and you were flung into a unknown and bleak future.
Varuhn is no more, his soul banished from the world beyond a point where even the Emperor could retrieve it. Breirhold is safe for now.
Destroy Varuhn the Defiler
Have you slain Varuhn
The Void Assault

In the Last Refuge Outskirts head north east towards the Council Chambers.

The people of Last Refuge have long cowered beneath the ground. They were safe from the Void that ravaged the surface world, but that safety is no more, and the Void pours into Last Refuge.

If you wish to help, you should head northeast towards the Council Chambers and seek the guidance of the elders.
Head north east towards the Council Chambers.

In the Last Refuge Outskirts help Elder Erza repel the voidforms.

The people of Last Refuge have long cowered beneath the ground. They were safe from the Void that ravaged the surface world, but that safety is no more, and the Void pours into Last Refuge.

You headed towards the council chambers to seek the guidance of the elders. On your way, you found Elder Erza under attack by void forms, help him hold them off.
Help Elder Erza repel the voidforms.

In the Last Refuge Outskirts go through the portal to reach the Council Chambers.

The people of Last Refuge have long cowered beneath the ground. They were safe from the Void that ravaged the surface world, but that safety is no more, and the Void pours into Last Refuge.

You headed towards the council chambers to seek the guidance of the elders but found Elder Erza under attack. 

After you helped him repel the void forms, Erza opened a portal for you. Go through it to reach the Council Chambers.
Take the elevator to the Council Chambers

Meet with Elder Gaspar in the Council Chambers.

The people of Last Refuge have long cowered beneath the ground. They were safe from the Void that ravaged the surface world, but that safety is no more, and the Void pours into Last Refuge.

You headed towards the council chambers to seek the guidance of the elders but found Elder Erza under attack. 

After you helped him repel the void forms, Erza opened a portal for you. Go through it to reach the Council Chambers. Now speak with the leader of Last Refuge, Elder Gaspar.
Meet with Elder Gaspar in the Council Chambers.

The people of Last Refuge have long cowered beneath the ground. They were safe from the Void that ravaged the surface world, but that safety is no more, and the Void pours into Last Refuge.

In the initial chaos, you headed northeast to reach the Council Chambers and sought the guidance of the elders.
Armory Aid

Captain Ansel has tasked you to find his men down in the Armory and provide aid.

The Armoury lies further down the city, below the Upper District. The entrance is at the eastern end of the Precipice.
Find the Armory.

Captain Ansel has tasked you to find his men down in the Armory and provide aid.

You reached the Armoury to find that Void creatures have broken through the barricade! Survive!
Survive the attacking Void!

Captain Ansel has tasked you to find his men down in the Armory and provide aid.

You survived the Void assault, now talk to the other survivor.
Speak with the surviving guard in the Armoury.

Captain Ansel has tasked you to find his men down in the Armory and provide aid.

The surviving guard asked you to find the Quartermaster. He may have hidden away within the Armory.
Find the Quartermaster in the Armory.

Captain Ansel has tasked you to find his men down in the Armory and provide aid.

You have found the Quartermaster and assured his safety. He will now be able to bring supplies back to the Council Chambers. Return to Captain Ansel and report your progress.
Return to Captain Ansel in the Council Chambers.

Captain Ansel has tasked you to find his men down in the Armory and provide aid.

You have aided in the rescue of men and supplies from the Armoury. Captain Ansel and the surviving guards may now have a chance of defending the Council Chambers!
Erza's Ledger

In the Upper Distrcit find Erza's Study.

Elder Erza, the man who guided you from the Outskirts of Last Refuge to the safety of the Council Chamber, has tasked you with retrieving his Ledger.

He says that his Ledger can be found within his library back in the Last Archives not far from here, but beware, it appears that the void may have overtaken that part of the city.
Find Elder Erza's Ledger in Erza's Library

Return to Elder Erza in the Council Chambers.

Elder Erza, the man who guided you from the Outskirts of Last Refuge to the safety of the Council Chamber, has tasked you with retrieving his Ledger.

You have retrieved the Ledger from Erza's study now you must make your way back to him!
Return to Elder Erza in the Council Chambers

Elder Erza, the man who guided you from the Outskirts of Last Refuge to the safety of the Council Chamber, had tasked you with retrieving his Ledger.

After retrieving his Ledger, Erza graciously parted with one of his possessions as a gift to you. Could there be a kinder soul?

Elder Erza, the man who guided you from the Outskirts of Last Refuge to the safety of the Council Chamber, has tasked you with retrieving his Ledger.

You decided to give the ledger to Artem the Gambler rather than its rightful owner. How could you?
Saving Last Refuge

Travel to the Precipice beyond the Last Archives.

The Shard of the Epoch was taken by Elder Pannion in a desperate attempt to fend off the Void. Without the Shard, this will be the end of the last bastion of humanity.

Pursue Elder Pannion who has taken the Shard with him to face the Void down in the Lower District.
Reach the Precipice.

Find Elder Pannion in the Lower District.

Elder Gaspar has asked for your help. The Shard of the Epoch was taken by Elder Pannion in a desperate attempt to fend off the Void. The Shard had been the sole force protecting the city and, if it is not retrieved, there will be no way to protect the survivors in the Council Chambers. Without the Shard, this will be the end of the last bastion of humanity. 

Pursue Elder Pannion who has taken the Shard with him to face the Void down in the Lower District. Travel to the Upper District beyond the Last Archives.
Reach the Upper District.

Travel to the Armoury beyond the Precipice.

The Shard of the Epoch was taken by Elder Pannion in a desperate attempt to fend off the Void. Without the Shard, this will be the end of the last bastion of humanity.


Pursue Elder Pannion who has taken the Shard with him to face the Void down in the Lower District.
Reach the Armoury.

Travel to the Lower District beyond the Armoury.


The Shard of the Epoch was taken by Elder Pannion in a desperate attempt to fend off the Void. Without the Shard, this will be the end of the last bastion of humanity.


Pursue Elder Pannion who has taken the Shard with him to face the Void down in the Lower District.
Reach the Lower District located beyond the Armoury.

Find Elder Pannion in the Lower District.

The Shard of the Epoch was taken by Elder Pannion in a desperate attempt to fend off the Void. Without the Shard, this will be the end of the last bastion of humanity.

You have reached the Lower District. Find Elder Pannion and bring him and the Shard back to the Council Chambers.
Find Elder Pannion in the Lower District.

Drive back the Void in the Lower District.

You have found Elder Pannion, but he transformed into a massive Void creature. Survive!
Drive back the Void!

Pick up the Shard in the Lower District.

Elder Pannion is lost to the Void. Elder Pannion's Void-corrupted body attacked you. Now that his husk has been vanquished the Shard of the Epoch lays before you.
Pick up the Shard.

Return to the Council Chambers - use the waypoint in the Lower District.

You have picked up the Shard left by Elder Pannion. Now you must find a way to bring it back to Elder Gaspar in the Council Chambers. The path behind you is rapidly filling with more Void, but there may be a waypoint ahead you could use.
Continue past the Lower District to the Sheltered Wood.

Return to Elder Gaspar in the Council Chambers.

You survived fighting the Husk of Pannion and retrieved the Shard. Return the Shard to Elder Gaspar in the Council Chambers and tell him of Elder Pannion's fate.
Return to Elder Gaspar in the Council Chambers.

Elder Gaspar has told you of the severity of the threat facing Last Refuge. If the Shard of the Epoch is not retrieved there will be no way to protect the survivors in the Council Chambers, and even if they found a way to deter the Void, the absence of the Shard would signal the end of the last bastion of humanity. 

You put Elder Pannion out of his misery and reported back to Elder Gaspar.
The Ruined World

Travel from the Council Chambers to the Surface.

The people of Last Refuge have entrusted you with their Shard - a fragment of an immensely powerful artifact known as the Epoch. This shard is their only means of defending themselves from the Void. Their only hope for survival is for you to find and restore the pieces of the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra and find a way to use its power.

Elder Gaspar has told you of a passage to the surface that lies at the end of the Sheltered Wood.
Reach the Surface beyond the Sheltered Wood.

Travel from the Surface to the Ruins of Welryn.

The people of Last Refuge have entrusted their Shard to you. Their only hope for survival is for you to find and restore the pieces of the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra and find a way to use its power.

You have reached the Surface, but the Temple is still out of reach. Elder Gaspar told you that it lies to the east, beyond the city of Welryn.
Find the city of Welryn.

Travel from the Surface to the Ruins of Welryn.

The people of Last Refuge have entrusted their Shard to you. Their only hope for survival is for you to find and restore the pieces of the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra and find a way to use its power.

You have reached the Surface, but the Temple is still out of reach. Elder Gaspar told you that it lies to the east, beyond the city of Welryn.
Assist Welryn's denizens

Travel from the Ruins of Welryn to the Shattered Valley.

The people of Last Refuge have entrusted their Shard to you. Their only hope for survival is for you to find and restore the pieces of the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra to the east.

You found the city of Welryn that Elder Gaspar described only to see it in ruins. Fight your way through the Ruins of Welryn to continue your path to the east.
Travel east beyond the Ruins of Welryn.

Travel from the Shattered Valley to the Courtyard.

The only hope for the people of Last Refuge is for you to find the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra to the east. 

You have escaped the Ruins of Welryn. The Temple must be close now. Continue traveling east.
Find the Temple of Eterra.

Defeat the Temple's Guardian in the Courtyard.

The only hope for the people of Last Refuge is for you to find the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra to the east. 

You found what appears to be the entrance to the Temple of Eterra, but a massive statue at the gates has begun to move and steps towards you. Defend yourself!
Defeat the Temple's Guardian in the Courtyard.

The people of Last Refuge entrusted you with their Shard - a fragment of an immensely powerful artifact known as the Epoch. This shard is their only means of defending themselves from the Void. Their only hope for survival is for you to find and restore the pieces of the Epoch deep within the Temple of Eterra and find a way to use its power.

You traversed the Shattered Valley and its foreboding ruins to find the entrance of what must be the long deserted Temple of Eterra.
Pannion's Students

Pannion's Library lies just behind Apprentice Farron, enter to investigate the situation.

You found Apprentice Farron in the Last Archives. Elder Pannion tasked him with guarding the entrance to his library to make sure none of the other students left. Farron has asked you to enter the library and inform him of what is going on.
Investigate Pannion's Library

In Pannion's Library defeat Pannion's students.

You found Apprentice Farron in the Last Archives. Elder Pannion tasked him with guarding the entrance to his library to make sure none of the other students left. Farron has asked you to enter the library and inform him of what is going on.

Pannion's students are corrupted by the Void! Put them out of their misery.
Defeat Pannion's Students

You found Apprentice Farron in the Last Archives. Elder Pannion tasked him with guarding the entrance to his library to make sure none of the other students left. Farron has asked you to enter the library and inform him of what is going on.

You've defeated Pannion's corrupted students, return to Apprentice Farron to report what you found.
Return to Apprentice Farron in the Last Archive

You found Apprentice Farron in the Last Archives. Elder Pannion tasked him with guarding the entrance to his library to make sure none of the other students left. Farron has asked you to enter the library and inform him of what is going on.

You've defeated Pannion's corrupted students and returned to Apprentice Farron. You've earned Apprentice Farron's thanks, but you wonder if he wouldn't have preferred not knowing what happened.
The Sane Cultist

Enter the Welryn Catacombs and find the sane cultists.

In the Ruins of Welryn, there was a human dressed the same as the cultists that fill the streets of the city. Yet, unlike the others, she appeared to be clear of mind. The woman informed you that there are others like her hiding below the city and asked you to aid in their escape.
Find the Sane Cultist's family in the Welryn Catacombs.

Fight off the Void Cultists in the Welryn Catacombs!

The woman lied to you! She told you that there are others like her, sane cultists, hiding below the city and asked you to aid in their escape. But instead of sane humans you found an ambush!
Defeat the Void Cultist Ambush.

Return to the sane cultist in the Ruins of Welryn.

The woman asked you to aid in the escape of other sane cultists hiding below the city. But instead of sane humans you found an ambush!

You managed to fight off the ambushing Void Cultists. Return to the woman and confront her.
Confront the "Sane" Cultist in the Ruins of Welryn.

In the Ruins of Welryn, there was a human dressed the same as the cultists that fill the streets of the city. Yet, unlike the others, she appeared to be clear of mind. The woman informed you that there are others like her hiding below the city and asked you to aid in their escape.

The woman turned out to be a liar and attempted to lure you into a trap. You fought off the ambush and returned to confront her, only for her to transform into a Void monstrosity. These ruins seem to hold only madness.
The Lesser Refuge

Look for signs of the Lesser Refuge on the Forsaken Trail.

There are records in the Archives that date back to when humanity was first fleeing the void. Among these records are reports of similar attempts by another group to hide within a cave system not too far from Last Refuge. Geova the Archivist has asked you to look for signs of this Lesser Refuge while you are exploring the surface.
Find signs of the Lesser Refuge in the Shattered Valley.

From the Shattered Valley, explore the caves until you find the Lost Refuge.

Geova the Archivist has asked you to look for signs of the Lesser Refuge mentioned in ancient reports.

You have found what appears to be an entrance into a cave system that may fit Geova's description. Explore the caves for signs of the Lesser Refuge.
Explore the caves under the Shattered Valley.

Investigate the ruined building in the Lost Refuge.

Deep within this cave system, you found the remains of what must have been the Lesser Refuge that Geova described. There was clearly some attempt to live in these caves, but the Void has long since consumed the place. Nearby you can see an old chest.
Investigate the ruined building in the Lost Refuge.

Among the old records dating back to when humanity was first fleeing the void, there are reports of attempts by another group to hide within a cave system not too far from Last Refuge. Geova the Archivist has asked you to look for signs of this Lesser Refuge while you are exploring the surface.

You found a cave system that met Geova's description, but your exploration revealed only ruins long consumed by the Void. However, you did find an old tome protected by faint magic.
The Fate of the Refuge

Return to Geova in the Council Chambers.

In your search for the Lesser Refuge, you found a cave system that met Geova's description. Unfortunately, your exploration revealed only ruins long consumed by the Void. However, you did find an old tome protected by faint magic.

By reading this tome you discovered the history of this place and their desire to be remembered as great, though they are now remembered as lesser. Return to Geova with this information.
Return to Geova in the Council Chambers.

In your search for the Lesser Refuge, you found a cave system that met Geova's description. Unfortunately, your exploration revealed only ruins long consumed by the Void. However, you did find an old tome protected by faint magic.

By reading the tome, you discovered the history of this place and its people. You returned to Geova with your findings and preserved their memory.

In your search for the Lesser Refuge, you found a cave system that met Geova's description. Unfortunately, your exploration revealed only ruins long consumed by the Void. However, you did find an old tome protected by faint magic.

By reading the tome, you discovered the history of this place and its people. You returned to Geova with your findings and insisted that they wanted to be remembered as Rat City for some reason.
Artem's Offer

Artem the Gambler approached you after seeing that you had agreed to recover Elder Erza's ledger. He proposed that you bring it to him rather than to Erza.

Elder Erza's ledger lies within Erza's Library in the Last Archives.
Look for Elder Erza's Ledger in Erza's Study

Artem the Gambler approached you after seeing that you had agreed to recover Elder Erza's ledger. He proposed that you bring it to him rather than to Erza.

You have found Erza's Ledger now you must return to the Council Chambers and decide whether you want to give it to Erza or Artem.
Bring Erza's Ledger to Artem in the Council Chambers

Artem the Gambler approached you after seeing that you had agreed to recover Elder Erza's ledger. He proposed that you bring it to him rather than to Erza.

You brought the ledger to Artem rather than Erza and thus earned his gratitude.

Artem the Gambler approached you after seeing that you had agreed to recover Elder Erza's ledger. He proposed that you bring it to him rather than to Erza.

You have decided to bring the ledger to Erza as promised. Artem seems shaken by this, but he'll live unless the Void makes it to the Council Chambers before you can stop it.
A Study in Time

Find the gates to the Great College in Welryn and gain entry.


Ansella at the Outcast Camp has confiscated keys to the Great College of Welryn from an Imperial Scholar. She has given them to you and asked you to investigate what the Immortal Empire is doing there.
Gain entry to the Great College in Welryn.
YouΓÇÖve entered the Great College, just like Ansella asked. Investigate the interior to find out as much as you can about what the Immortal Empire is up to.
Investigate the Great College of Welryn.

Travel forward in time to the Ruined Era to investigate the Great College for clues in what is in that time the Ruins of Welryn.

There is a door to the upper levels of the Great College, but it is sealed shut in this time. The Imperial Officer on the other side insists that the OfficerΓÇÖs Crest on his person is the only way to open the door. Perhaps by traveling to the future, you may find a way to open the door, and you can investigate for Ansella.
Use the Time Rift to investigate the Great College in the Ruined Era.

Go back in time to the Imperial Era and return to the Great College in Welryn.

In the Ruined Era, in the Ruins of Welryn, you found that the Sealed Door was broken long ago. Beyond the door, you found the remains of the Imperial Officer that had guarded it along with his OfficerΓÇÖs Crest. The OfficerΓÇÖs Crest may still work on the door in the past.
Use the Time Rift to return to the Sealed Door in the Great College of Welryn.

Investigate the Upper Floor of the Great College of Welryn.

It worked! Much to the Imperial OfficerΓÇÖs confusion and dismay, the OfficerΓÇÖs Crest from the Ruined Era dispelled the magic on the Sealed Door. Continue your investigation of the Great College and give Ansella a report of what you find.
Investigate the Upper Floor of the Great College of Welryn.

Return to Ansella at the Outcast Camp to report your findings.

Ansella at the Outcast Camp has confiscated keys to the Great College of Welryn from an Imperial Scholar. She gave them to you and asked you to investigate what the Immortal Empire is doing there.

In the upper floors of the Great College, you found the remains of humans who were used for experimentation. Return to Ansella at the Outcast Camp and tell her what you saw.
Return to Ansella at the Outcast Camp.

Ansella at the Outcast Camp has confiscated keys to the Great College of Welryn from an Imperial Scholar. She gave them to you and asked you to investigate what the Immortal Empire is doing there.

Through the use of time travel, you managed to investigate the Great College of Welryn and free the prisoners trapped within, earning AnsellaΓÇÖs respect and gratitude.
The Ruined Temple

Explore the Temple of Eterra.

When you entered the Temple of Eterra, the voice of the Epoch called out soundlessly. The Shard is close, and it pulls at the pieces you carry to become whole again.

You have entered the ruined Temple of Eterra, brave its depths and search for the Shard.
Explore the Temple of Eterra and find the Lotus Halls.

Examine the bridge in the Temple of Eterra. 

When you entered the Temple of Eterra, the voice of the Epoch called out soundlessly. The Shard is close, and it pulls at the pieces you carry to become whole again.

You discovered the Lotus Halls and a strange device that seems connected to an inactive bridge.
Examine the bridge to the lowest level of the Temple of Eterra

Find and return the missing parts of the bridge in the Temple of Eterra. 

When you entered the Temple of Eterra, the voice of the Epoch called out soundlessly. The Shard is close, and it pulls at the pieces you carry to become whole again.

You discovered the Lotus Halls and a strange device that seems connected to an inactive bridge. The devices seem to be missing a crucial part that might make them operational again. The Shards in your possession continue to call to you, urging you to rooms deeper within the Lotus Halls.
Find one of the missing parts of the bridge device so it can be reactivated

Find and return the missing parts of the bridge in the Temple of Eterra. 

When you entered the Temple of Eterra, the voice of the Epoch called out soundlessly. The Shard is close, and it pulls at the pieces you carry to become whole again.

You discovered the Lotus Halls and a strange device that seems connected to an inactive bridge. The devices seem to be missing a crucial part that might make them operational again. The Shards in your possession continue to call to you, urging you to rooms deeper within the Lotus Halls.
Reactivate the right bridge device
Reactivate the bridge device

Use the bridge to reach the Sanctum Bastille.


When you entered the Temple of Eterra, the voice of the Epoch called out soundlessly. The Shard is close, and it pulls at the pieces you carry to become whole again.


You discovered the Lotus Halls and a strange device that seems connected to an inactive bridge. After returning the missing parts, a bridge that leads deeper into the temple has become active. Venture deeper and find the last Shard.
Enter the Sanctum Bastille.

Approach the final Shard in the Sanctum Bastille and form the Epoch.

In a chamber near the bottom of the Temple of Eterra is the Sanctum Bastille. As you approach the center, the Shards you carry resonate violently. Rejoin the Shards and form the Epoch!
Search the Sanctum Bastille for the last shard of the Epoch

Defeat the Emperor's Remains in the Sanctum Bastille.

Before you could form the Epoch, Void began rushing into the chamber, and from the darkness rose the beast you encountered back in Last Refuge: the Omen of Silence!
Defeat the Emperor's Remains!

Speak with Elder Gaspar in the End of Time.

Defeating the Emperor's Remains only slowed the Void; it continued to pour into the room. When defeat seemed imminent, the Shards began to shine with a brilliant light, pushing back the darkness and pulling you away. You have awoken somewhere beyond explanation, but even here, you find a familiar face.
Speak with Elder Gaspar in the upper terrace of the End of Time.

When you entered the Temple of Eterra, the voice of the Epoch called out soundlessly. The Shard is close, and it pulled at the pieces you carried to become whole again.


You explored the depths of the ruined Temple of Eterra, following the soundless voice. Eventually, you found the Epoch's chamber only to be attacked by a flood of Void lead by the Emperor's Remains. Defeating the Emperor's Remains only slowed the Void, it continued to pour into the room until the Shards pushed back the darkness with a brilliant light and brought you to the End of Time. Here, you spoke with Elder Gaspar and learned that this place is an inevitable endpoint for all timelines beset by the Void.

Form the Epoch in the Sanctum Bastille and enter the Time Rift.

Defeating the Emperor's Remains only slowed the Void; it continues to pour into the room. The Epoch may be the only hope now!
Form the Epoch and enter the Time Rift.
The Immortal Empire

Speak with Elder Gaspar.

You have found yourself in a place known as the End of Time - the shattered remains of many timelines, including yours. Elder Gaspar has told you that there may be a way to save the world you came from. Speak with him again to learn more.
Speak with Gaspar in the End of Time.

Approach the Time Rift in the End of Time and travel to the Imperial Era.

In speaking with Elder Gaspar, you learned that every shattered timeline in the End of Time has been destroyed by the Void. The only way to save your time is to stop the Void before it starts. Based on the stories of countless travelers, the most likely origin of the Void has to do with the dark ambitions of the Immortal Emperor. Feeding off the living, this Empire lives in an empowered state of undeath. Travel to the Imperial Era to stop him.
Use the Time Rift to travel to the Imperial Era.

Speak with the Outcast Queen in the Outcast Camp.

Based on the stories of countless travelers, the most likely origin of the Void has to do with the dark ambitions of the Immortal Emperor and you have traveled to the Imperial Era to stop him.

Your first step in your dealing with the Immortal Empire is to establish contact with the living rebels and their leader, the Outcast Queen.
Speak with the Outcast Queen in the Outcast Camp.

From the Outcast Camp, travel west towards Welryn.

The Outcast Queen, leader of the living rebels who oppose the Immortal Emperor, has agreed to accept your aid in their fight. Her first task for you is to rescue one of their mages who has been captured by the Immortal Empire. This mage knows of a secret way into the Immortal Citadel, which may be instrumental in the Empire's defeat. He was last seen west of the Outcast Camp.
Travel west to Welryn.

Search Welryn for signs of the mage!

To aid in the fight against the Immortal Empire, the Outcast Queen has tasked you with rescuing one of their mages who has been captured. Your search has brought you back to the city of Welryn, more intact than you had seen it in the Ruined Era, but still filled with danger as the Empire's forces swarm its streets. Fight your way through and search for the mage.
Search Welryn for signs of the Outcast Mage.

Defeat the Immortal Empire commander in Welryn.

After searching the streets of Welryn, you found a group of living prisoners cornered by an Immortal Empire commander. Defeat the commander and save the remaining prisoners!
Defeat the Imperial Commander!

Speak with the surviving prisoner in Welryn.

You've defeated the Immortal Empire commander, but you were too late to save all of them. Only one prisoner survived. The living prisoner is not the mage you are looking for. Speak with her to discover if she knows where he might be.
Speak with the Outcast Prisoner in Welryn.

Find the Outcasts in the Risen Lake.

The surviving prisoner that you saved from the Immortal Empire commander knows of the mage you seek. His name is Alric. He was shipped off in the last transport of prisoners and she does not know where he could have been taken. However, he had given her a ring that he claimed was enchanted to hold a connection to him. One of the Outcasts hiding in the nearby Risen Lake may be able to use this ring to divine Alric's location. Take the ring to the Seer.
Find the Outcast Hideout in Risen Lake

Speak with the Outcast Seer in the Risen Lake.

After traveling west of Welryn, you entered the Risen Lake, or at least, it used to be a lake. The living had made their homes atop raised stilts and platforms over the waters. But now, all that is left is a swampy marsh, filled with the remains of those who lived here and the walkways are filled with the forces of the Immortal Empire.

After fighting your way through the undead you have found a corner of the Risen Lake where one of the Outcasts has hidden away. She appears to be the Seer that the prisoner told you of - the one who can help you locate Alric.
Show Alric's ring to the Outcast Seer in the Risen Lake.

Defend the Outcast Seer in the Risen Lake!

The Outcast Seer took Alric's ring and began to use the enchanted connection to him to divine where he is being held. As her spell began to take shape, you heard the sound of undead approaching from behind you. The Immortal Empire followed you and has found the Outcast Seer. Defend her as she finishes the ritual!
Defend the Seer!

Speak to the Outcast Seer in the Risen Lake once again.

You managed to fight off the Immortal Empire's attack. After the fight, you see that the Outcast Seer is winded and in poor shape; one of the stray attacks from the undead must have hit her. She is wounded, but alive. Return to her and ask about the results of her divination to find Alric.
Return to the Seer in the Risen Lake.

The Outcast Seer used her magic to locate Alric. To the west is the coastal city Thetima, and within its harbor is Admiral Harton's Dreadnought, a massive warship of the Immortal Empire. Alric is being held in the hold of the Dreadnought. You may be his only hope.
The End of Time

Travel to the different notable places within the End of Time.

You have found yourself in a place known as the End of Time - the shattered remains of many timelines, including yours. You found the familiar face of Elder Gaspar who tells you that there may be a way to save the world you came from. But first, he instructs you to look around and familiarize yourself with this place outside of time.
Find the Arena
Find the Bazaar
Find the Monolith of Fate
Find the Gate of Memorium
Find the Chronomancer

Go back to Elder Gaspar. 

You have seen the various notable sections of the End of Time. It seems to be a place of inevitable fate. Speak with Elder Gaspar again to determine what to do next.
Return to Elder Gaspar

You have found yourself in a place known as the End of Time - the shattered remains of many timelines, including yours. You found the familiar face of Elder Gaspar who tells you that there may be a way to save the world you came from. But first, he instructs you to look around and familiarize yourself with this place outside of time.

You have seen the various notable sections of the End of Time and spoken with Elder Gaspar. You are now ready to continue your quest and save the world!
ActorTest
Walk to chest.
Walk back to NPC. Don't talk to bear.
A Long Detour

In the Forest Trail travel east to the Traveler's Camp.
